title: The Old Bark Hut
content:
آ آ آ آ Oh, my name is Bob the Swagman, before you all I stand,
آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½ve had many ups and downs while travelling through the land.
آ آ آ آ I once was well-to-do, my boys, but now I am stumped up,
آ آ آ آ And Iï؟½m forced to go on rations in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut.آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Iï؟½m forced to go on rations in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ Ten pounds of flour, ten pounds of beef, some sugar and some tea,
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s all they give to a hungry man, until the Seventh Day.
آ آ آ آ If you donï؟½t be moighty sparing, youï؟½ll go with a hungry gutï؟½
آ آ آ آ For thatï؟½s one of the great misfortunes in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ For thatï؟½s one of the great misfortunes in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ The bucket you boil your beef in has to carry water, too,
آ آ آ آ And theyï؟½ll say youï؟½re getting mighty flash if you should ask for two.
آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve a billy, and a pint pot, and a broken-handled cup,
آ آ آ آ And they all adorn the table in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ And they all adorn the table in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ Faith, the table is not made of wood, as many you have seenï؟½
آ آ آ آ For if I had one half so good, Iï؟½d think myself sereneï؟½
آ آ آ آ ï؟½Tis only an old sheet of barkï؟½God knows when it was cutï؟½
آ آ آ آ It was blown from off the rafters of the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ It was blown from off the rafters of the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ And of furniture, thereï؟½s no such thing, ï؟½twas never in the place,
آ آ آ آ Except the stool I sit uponï؟½and thatï؟½s an old gin case.
آ آ آ آ It does us for a safe as well, but you must keep it shut,
آ آ آ آ Or the flies would make it canter round the old hark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut.آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Or the flies would make it canter round the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ If you should leave it open, and the flies should find your meat,
آ آ آ آ Theyï؟½ll scarcely leave a single piece thatï؟½s fit for man to eat.
آ آ آ آ But you mustnï؟½t curse, nor grumbleï؟½what wonï؟½t fatten will fill upï؟½
آ آ آ آ For whatï؟½s out of sight is out of mind in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  For whatï؟½s out of sight is out of mind in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ In the summer time, when the weatherï؟½s warm, this hut is nice and cool,
آ آ آ آ And youï؟½ll find the gentle breezes blowing in through every hole.
آ آ آ آ You can leave the old door open, or you can leave it shut,
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s no fear of suffocation in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Thereï؟½s no fear of suffocation in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ In the winter timeï؟½preserve us allï؟½to live in thereï؟½s a treat
آ آ آ آ Especially when itï؟½s raining hard, and blowing wind and sleet.

آ آ آ آ The rain comes down the chimney, and your meat is black with sootï؟½
آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s a substitute for pepper in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Thatï؟½s a substitute for pepper in an old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ Iï؟½ve seen the rain come in this hut just like a perfect flood,
آ آ آ آ Especially through that great big hole where once the table stood.
آ آ آ آ Thereï؟½s not a blessed spot, me boys, where you could lay your nut,
آ آ آ آ But the rain is sure to find you in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ But the rain is sure to find you in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ So beside the fire I make me bed, and there I lay me down,
آ آ آ آ And think myself as happy as the king that wears a crown.
آ آ آ آ But as youï؟½d be dozing off to sleep a flea will wake you up,
آ آ آ آ Which makes you curse the vermin in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Which makes you curse the vermin in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ Faith, such flocks of fleas you never saw, they are so plump and fat,
آ آ آ آ And if you make a grab at one, heï؟½ll spit just like a cat.
آ آ آ آ Last night they got my pack of cards, and were fighting for the cutï؟½
آ آ آ آ I thought the devil had me in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ I thought the devil had me in the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ So now, my friends, Iï؟½ve sung my song, and that as well as I could,
آ آ آ آ And I hope the ladies present wonï؟½t think my language rude,
آ آ آ آ And all ye younger people, in the days when you grow up,
آ آ آ آ Remember Bob the Swagman, and the old bark hut.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ In an old bark hut. In an old bark hut.
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ Remember Bob the Swagman, and the old bark hut.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
